failure_modes: Gradient signal too weak (mean magnitude ~0.000003) to improve FSCC beyond feature-only
Gate activation at 50% (median threshold) is not regime-aware — effectively random edge selection
FSCC delta +0.09pp not significant (p=0.575), win rate 0.47 below 0.6 threshold
claim_recommendation: The selective gate prevents MCGC degradation (+1.9pp LFS vs -2.5pp for raw MCGC) but cannot create meaningful FSCC gains. The bottleneck is gradient signal quality, not threshold selection. The paper should either (1) reframe around the gate-as-regularizer story (preventing gradient noise from hurting), or (2) abandon the training-dynamics claim and focus on the engineering contribution of selective gating.
